message:
feat(snapshot_engine): tick 204 mark_shutdown_with_cx session_id read_lock cx-threaded (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

SnapshotEngine::mark_shutdown_with_cx (snapshot_engine.rs L1426)
was in the "mostly cx-threaded with one ambient hole" shape:

- pre-flight `cx.checkpoint()` ✅
- post-spawn-blocking `cx.checkpoint()` ✅
- session_id read-lock: `self.session_id.read().await` ❌ ambient

The ambient `read()` picks up cx via Cx::current() thread-local
fallback. The `session_id` RwLock is the same one used by
`ensure_session_with_cx` (L1260) — which correctly uses
`read_with_cx(cx)`. So the orphan was purely a missed swap.

Tick 204 routes the read-lock through `read_with_cx(cx)`. Same
surgical pattern as tick 198 (notifications mute-check) and
tick 200/201 (ipc next_line).

Verification: 92 / 92 snapshot_engine:: tests pass under
--features asupersync-runtime via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang,
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target), including
mark_shutdown_with_cx_is_cancel_aware and
run_periodic_with_cx_mid_flight_cancel_exits_quickly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
